description:
You are a deployment engineer rolling out a minor configuration update for a home-directory service instance.  
The service’s working directory is  

/home/user/service_cfg  

and it already contains the file  

/home/user/service_cfg/service.conf  

with the following exact contents (including line-breaks):

# Application Service Configuration  
version=1.8  
enable_new_ui=no  

Goal  
1. Safely back up the existing configuration.  
2. Update two key/value pairs inside service.conf.  
3. Leave an easily-parsable proof-of-work log that automated tests can check.

Detailed requirements  

A. Create a backup  
• In the same directory, create service.conf.bak.  
• The backup file must be byte-for-byte identical to the original, unmodified version of service.conf.

B. Modify service.conf in-place so that, after you are finished, it contains exactly (including line-breaks and the leading comment line):

# Application Service Configuration  
version=2.0  
enable_new_ui=yes  

C. Produce a deployment log  
• Ensure the directory /home/user/service_cfg/logs exists (create it if necessary).  
• Inside that directory write (or overwrite) the file /home/user/service_cfg/logs/deploy.log so that it contains exactly two lines and nothing else:

BACKUP_OK  
UPDATED_to_2.0  

Formatting notes for deploy.log  
• Each of the two lines must end with a single UNIX newline (LF, “\n”).  
• No extra blank lines or whitespace are allowed before, after, or between the two lines.  

Verification expectations (what automated tests look for)  
1. /home/user/service_cfg/service.conf.bak exists and is an exact copy of the *original* file.  
2. /home/user/service_cfg/service.conf has the three lines shown in section B and no others.  
3. /home/user/service_cfg/logs/deploy.log exists and contains precisely the two lines listed in section C.  

You do not need super-user privileges; everything happens in /home/user.  
Typical shell utilities (cp, mkdir, echo, etc.) are sufficient; the entire job can be completed in roughly 3–4 commands.  
Do **not** print the contents of files to the console as part of the solution—only create/modify the files on disk as described.
truth:
Initial filesystem state (pre-task):

Directory /home/user/service_cfg/
├── service.conf  (contents shown below)

# Application Service Configuration
version=1.8
enable_new_ui=no

Directory /home/user/service_cfg/logs/ does *not* initially exist.

Expected final state (post-task):

1. /home/user/service_cfg/service.conf.bak  
   • File exists.  
   • md5sum must equal md5sum of the original service.conf provided above.

2. /home/user/service_cfg/service.conf  
   • Exact contents:

# Application Service Configuration
version=2.0
enable_new_ui=yes

3. /home/user/service_cfg/logs/deploy.log  
   • Exact contents (including trailing newlines):

BACKUP_OK
UPDATED_to_2.0

(no extra spaces or blank lines)

Automated test checklist (pseudo):

assert file_exists('/home/user/service_cfg/service.conf.bak')
assert md5('/home/user/service_cfg/service.conf.bak') == md5(original_conf)

expected_conf = "# Application Service Configuration\nversion=2.0\nenable_new_ui=yes\n"
assert file_contents('/home/user/service_cfg/service.conf') == expected_conf

expected_log = "BACKUP_OK\nUPDATED_to_2.0\n"
assert file_contents('/home/user/service_cfg/logs/deploy.log') == expected_log
